title: Trump’s Armada Is Getting in Place. Now He Must Decide What to Do With Iran.
title_cn: 特朗普的舰队已就位。现在他必须决定如何处置伊朗。

paragraphs.cn: 2026年1月30日 美国东部时间晚上8:00

paragraphs.en: DUBAI—The U.S. military has assembled a formidable force in the Middle East within striking range of Iran. Now, President Trump must decide how to use it.
paragraphs.cn: 迪拜——美国军方在中东地区集结了一支强大的部队，处于伊朗的打击范围之内。现在，特朗普总统必须决定如何使用它。

paragraphs.en: As warships and planes reach the region in growing numbers, administration officials said they are debating whether the main aim is to go after Iran’s nuclear program, hit its ballistic missile arsenal, bring about the collapse of the government—or some combination of the three.
paragraphs.cn: 随着军舰和飞机越来越多地抵达该地区，政府官员表示，他们正在辩论主要目标是针对伊朗的核计划、打击其弹道导弹武库、促使政府垮台——或是这三者的某种组合。

paragraphs.en: Trump has asked aides for quick and decisive attack options that don’t risk a long-term war in the Middle East, officials said. The ideal option would be one that hits the regime hard enough that it has no choice but to accede to U.S. nuclear demands and lay off dissidents, they said.
paragraphs.cn: 官员们说，特朗普已要求助手提供快速而果断的攻击方案，且不冒在中东地区陷入长期战争的风险。他们表示，理想的选择是能够对该政权造成足够大的打击，使其别无选择，只能同意美国的核要求并停止镇压持不同政见者。

paragraphs.en: There have been discussions about a punishing bombing campaign that could topple Iran’s government, the officials said. Trump and his team have also weighed leveraging the threat of military force to extract diplomatic concessions from Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 官员们说，已经讨论过一场可能推翻伊朗政府的惩罚性轰炸行动。特朗普及其团队还权衡了利用军事威胁从伊朗获取外交让步的可能性。

paragraphs.en: What Trump decides will determine the shape of any military action. “The kind of things you’d want to do and the force packages you would need are very different,” said retired Vice Adm. Robert Murrett, a former Navy intelligence officer.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普的决定将决定任何军事行动的形态。“你想要做的事情和所需的兵力组合是非常不同的，”前海军情报官员、退役海军中将罗伯特·默雷特说。

paragraphs.en: A senior administration official said that while Trump has consistently said Iran can’t have a nuclear weapon, he is being purposefully ambiguous to keep his strategic objectives and military thinking secret.
paragraphs.cn: 一位高级政府官员表示，尽管特朗普一直声称伊朗不能拥有核武器，但他故意含糊其辞，以保守其战略目标和军事思想的秘密。

paragraphs.en: Speaking about the ships converging on the Middle East, Trump told reporters Friday in the Oval Office that “they have to float someplace. They might as well float near Iran.”
paragraphs.cn: 谈到汇聚在中东的船只，特朗普周五在椭圆形办公室告诉记者，“它们总得有个地方停泊。它们不妨停泊在伊朗附近。”

paragraphs.en: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Friday that Tehran was open to nuclear discussions but that the U.S. needed to stop issuing military threats.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格奇周五表示，德黑兰对核谈判持开放态度，但美国需要停止发出军事威胁。

paragraphs.en: Trump in his second term has ordered attacks in Yemen, Iran, Venezuela, Syria, Somalia and Nigeria, often without an Oval Office speech explaining his actions or seeking congressional authorization.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普在其第二任期内曾下令在也门、伊朗、委内瑞拉、叙利亚、索马里和尼日利亚发动袭击，通常没有在椭圆形办公室发表讲话解释其行动或寻求国会授权。

paragraphs.en: A January raid on Caracas was aimed at seizing Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro to stand trial. The airstrikes and missile attack on Iran in June targeted key nuclear sites with powerful bombs designed for penetrating such hardened targets.
paragraphs.cn: 1月份对加拉加斯的突袭旨在逮捕委内瑞拉总统尼古拉斯·马杜罗，使其受审。6月份对伊朗的空袭和导弹袭击瞄准了关键核设施，使用了旨在穿透此类坚固目标的强大炸弹。

paragraphs.en: Air Force Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs, and Trump’s other military advisers have helped the White House achieve notable tactical successes, often by using surprise operations developed over months that sought to avoid quagmires.
paragraphs.cn: 空军上将、参谋长联席会议主席丹·凯恩和特朗普的其他军事顾问帮助白宫取得了显著的战术成功，通常是通过使用经过数月开发的突袭行动，旨在避免陷入困境。

paragraphs.en: In Iran the president is facing an opponent that, though considerably weaker militarily than it was only a few years ago, remains capable of withstanding a major U.S. attack and of retaliating with missile and drone strikes on U.S. bases, warships, and allies in the region, including Israel.
paragraphs.cn: 在伊朗，总统正面临一个对手，尽管其军事实力比几年前明显减弱，但仍有能力承受美国的大规模袭击，并能对美国在该地区的基地、军舰和盟友（包括以色列）进行导弹和无人机报复性打击。

paragraphs.en: “There is no ‘shock and awe’ solution to the Iranian question,” said Danny Citrinowicz, a fellow at the Tel Aviv-based Institute for National Security Studies, alluding to the U.S. bombing campaign before the 2003 invasion of Iraq. “Anyone promising otherwise is probably mistaken.”
paragraphs.cn: “伊朗问题没有‘震慑’解决方案，”特拉维夫国家安全研究所研究员丹尼·西特里诺维奇说，他暗指2003年入侵伊拉克前的美国轰炸行动。“任何承诺其他结果的人可能都错了。”

paragraphs.en: Trump’s path toward new military confrontation with Tehran began when he vowed this month to come to the aid of antigovernment protesters demonstrating in Tehran and other cities.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普走向与德黑兰新军事对抗的道路始于他本月誓言要援助在德黑兰和其他城市示威的反政府抗议者。

paragraphs.en: At the time, there weren’t enough U.S. forces to the region to launch a sustained bombing campaign and defend U.S. bases and Middle East allies from Iran’s certain retaliation. Faced with bad options, Trump abruptly decided against military action.
paragraphs.cn: 当时，该地区没有足够的美国军队来发动持续的轰炸行动，并保护美国基地和中东盟友免受伊朗必然的报复。面对糟糕的选择，特朗普突然决定放弃军事行动。

paragraphs.en: Now he has more forces at his disposal. “We have a lot of very big, very powerful ships sailing to Iran right now. And it would be great if we didn’t have to use them,” he said Thursday, adding that he had communicated his demands to Tehran.
paragraphs.cn: 现在他有更多的兵力可供支配。“我们有很多非常大、非常强大的船只正在驶向伊朗。如果不必使用它们，那将是极好的，”他周四说，并补充说他已将自己的要求传达给德黑兰。

paragraphs.en: “I told them two things: Number one, no nuclear; and number two, stop killing protesters,” the president said.
paragraphs.cn: “我告诉他们两件事：第一，不要核武器；第二，停止杀害抗议者，”总统说。

paragraphs.en: Trump has received briefings on possible attack options developed in tandem by the White House and Pentagon. Among them is the so-called big plan, which would see the U.S. strike facilities of the regime and Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps in a large-scale bombing campaign, the officials said.
paragraphs.cn: 官员们说，特朗普已经听取了白宫和五角大楼协同制定的可能攻击方案的简报。其中包括所谓的“大计划”，即美国将对伊朗政权和伊斯兰革命卫队的设施进行大规模轰炸。

paragraphs.en: More limited options include strikes on symbolic regime targets, allowing space for attacks to ramp up if Iran, which denies seeking a nuclear weapon, doesn’t agree to a deal to Trump’s liking.
paragraphs.cn: 更有限的选择包括打击象征性的政权目标，为攻击升级留出空间，如果伊朗（否认寻求核武器）不同意特朗普满意的协议。

paragraphs.en: An operation targeting Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei like the one Trump ordered this month to capture Maduro using special forces teams would be far more difficult in Iran, which is extra vigilant about protecting its leadership and where the capital is far inland.
paragraphs.cn: 针对最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊的行动，就像特朗普本月下令使用特种部队抓捕马杜罗的行动一样，在伊朗将困难得多，因为伊朗对保护其领导层格外警惕，而且首都位于内陆深处。

paragraphs.en: Even if Khamenei is ousted, no one can say for sure whether the government that follows would be any more friendly, officials said. A senior IRGC member would likely assume control, some officials assess, which could preserve—or even deepen—the regime’s hard-line approach.
paragraphs.cn: 官员们说，即使哈梅内伊被罢免，也无法确定接替的政府是否会更友好。一些官员评估，一名高级伊斯兰革命卫队成员可能会掌权，这可能会保留——甚至加深——该政权的强硬路线。

paragraphs.en: Addressing a Senate committee Wednesday, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said it is an open question what would happen if Khamenei were removed and the regime fell. “I don’t think anyone can give you a simple answer as to what happens next in Iran,” Rubio said.
paragraphs.cn: 国务卿马尔科·卢比奥周三在参议院委员会发表讲话时表示，如果哈梅内伊被罢免且政权垮台，将会发生什么仍是一个悬而未决的问题。“我认为没有人能给你一个简单的答案，说明伊朗接下来会发生什么，”卢比奥说。

paragraphs.en: “Even if you defeat the regime really quickly, the day after matters,” said Vali Nasr, an Iran expert and former U.S. official now at Johns Hopkins University.
paragraphs.cn: “即使你很快击败了政权，第二天的事情也很重要，”伊朗问题专家、前美国官员、现约翰霍普金斯大学的瓦利·纳斯说。

paragraphs.en: Administration officials are using the threat of an attack to pressure Tehran to agree to talks on restricting its nuclear program, as well as limitations on its ballistic missiles and assistance to regional proxies.
paragraphs.cn: 政府官员正在利用攻击威胁向德黑兰施压，要求其同意就限制其核计划以及限制其弹道导弹和对地区代理人的援助进行谈判。

paragraphs.en: The White House is wary about getting drawn into unproductive negotiations. If Trump orders an attack instead, analysts said, none of the goals he has outlined is achievable through a swift round of airstrikes or missile attacks of the sort that the Pentagon appears to be readying.
paragraphs.cn: 白宫对卷入没有成效的谈判保持警惕。分析师表示，如果特朗普下令攻击，他所概述的任何目标都无法通过五角大楼似乎正在准备的那种迅速的空袭或导弹袭击来实现。

paragraphs.en: “He likes the application of military violence when it is quick, cheap and decisive,” said Justin Logan, director of defense and foreign policy studies at the Cato Institute in Washington. “The problem is you can’t do things quickly and cheaply and get decisive results.”
paragraphs.cn: “他喜欢快速、廉价且决定性的军事暴力应用，”华盛顿卡托研究所国防和外交政策研究主任贾斯汀·洛根说。“问题是，你无法快速、廉价地做事情并获得决定性的结果。”

paragraphs.en: Write to David S. Cloud at david.cloud@wsj.com and Alexander Ward at alex.ward@wsj.com
paragraphs.cn: 请发送邮件至 David S. Cloud (david.cloud@wsj.com) 和 Alexander Ward (alex.ward@wsj.com)

paragraphs.en: Appeared in the January 31, 2026, print edition as 'U.S., With Armada in Place, Weighs Action on Iran'.
paragraphs.cn: 刊登于2026年1月31日印刷版，标题为“美国舰队就位，权衡对伊朗行动”。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Inspiring fear or respect through being impressively large, powerful, intense, or capable.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 强大的；令人敬畏的；可怕的
vocabulary.example: The U.S. military has assembled a formidable force in the Middle East within striking range of Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: 美国军方在中东地区集结了一支强大的部队，处于伊朗的打击范围之内。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Gathered together in one place for a common purpose.
vocabulary.example: The U.S. military has assembled a formidable force in the Middle East within striking range of Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: 美国军方在中东地区集结了一支强大的部队，处于伊朗的打击范围之内。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The government in power; the period during which a particular government is in power.
vocabulary.example: As warships and planes reach the region in growing numbers, administration officials said they are debating whether the main aim is to go after Iran’s nuclear program...
vocabulary.example_cn: 随着军舰和飞机越来越多地抵达该地区，政府官员表示，他们正在辩论主要目标是针对伊朗的核计划...

vocabulary.meaning_en: Agree to a demand, request, or treaty.
vocabulary.example: The ideal option would be one that hits the regime hard enough that it has no choice but to accede to U.S. nuclear demands and lay off dissidents, they said.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们说，理想的选择是能够对该政权造成足够大的打击，使其别无选择，只能同意美国的核要求并停止镇压持不同政见者。

vocabulary.example: Trump and his team have also weighed leveraging the threat of military force to extract diplomatic concessions from Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普及其团队还权衡了利用军事威胁从伊朗获取外交让步的可能性。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Open to more than one interpretation; having a double meaning.
vocabulary.example: A senior administration official said that while Trump has consistently said Iran can’t have a nuclear weapon, he is being purposefully ambiguous to keep his strategic objectives and military thinking secret.
vocabulary.example_cn: 一位高级政府官员表示，尽管特朗普一直声称伊朗不能拥有核武器，但他故意含糊其辞，以保守其战略目标和军事思想的秘密。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The action of authorizing; official permission or sanction.
vocabulary.example: Trump in his second term has ordered attacks in Yemen, Iran, Venezuela, Syria, Somalia and Nigeria, often without an Oval Office speech explaining his actions or seeking congressional authorization.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普在其第二任期内曾下令在也门、伊朗、委内瑞拉、叙利亚、索马里和尼日利亚发动袭击，通常没有在椭圆形办公室发表讲话解释其行动或寻求国会授权。

vocabulary.meaning_en: An awkward, complex, or hazardous situation.
vocabulary.example: Air Force Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs, and Trump’s other military advisers have helped the White House achieve notable tactical successes, often by using surprise operations developed over months that sought to avoid quagmires.
vocabulary.example_cn: 空军上将、参谋长联席会议主席丹·凯恩和特朗普的其他军事顾问帮助白宫取得了显著的战术成功，通常是通过使用经过数月开发的突袭行动，旨在避免陷入困境。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Making an attack or assault in return for a similar attack.
vocabulary.example: In Iran the president is facing an opponent that, though considerably weaker militarily than it was only a few years ago, remains capable of withstanding a major U.S. attack and of retaliating with missile and drone strikes on U.S. bases, warships, and allies in the region, including Israel.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在伊朗，总统正面临一个对手，尽管其军事实力比几年前明显减弱，但仍有能力承受美国的大规模袭击，并能对美国在该地区的基地、军舰和盟友（包括以色列）进行导弹和无人机报复性打击。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A hostile or argumentative meeting or situation between opposing parties.
vocabulary.example: Trump’s path toward new military confrontation with Tehran began when he vowed this month to come to the aid of antigovernment protesters demonstrating in Tehran and other cities.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普走向与德黑兰新军事对抗的道路始于他本月誓言要援助在德黑兰和其他城市示威的反政府抗议者。
